questions.q: Mahadji Scindia was a leading chief of which Indian power?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia was one of the great chiefs of the Maratha confederacy.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia is credited with rebuilding Maratha power in the north after which disaster?
options: The sack of Delhi by Nadir Shah
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia rebuilt Maratha power in the north after the heavy defeat at the Third Battle of Panipat in 1761.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia became the dominant power at whose court in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia became the dominant force at the Mughal court in Delhi in the later 18th century.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia modernised his army along which model?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia famously reorganised and modernised his army on European lines with disciplined infantry and artillery.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia lived and was active during which century CE?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia was a statesman and general of the later 18th century CE.

questions.q: The Scindia (Shinde) house that Mahadji led was centred on which northern city?
questions.explanation: The Scindia dynasty led by Mahadji came to be centred on Gwalior in central-northern India.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia is best remembered as a Maratha what?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia is remembered as a leading Maratha chief and shrewd statesman.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia owed nominal allegiance to which Maratha authority?
questions.explanation: As a Maratha chief, Mahadji Scindia owed nominal allegiance to the Peshwa at Pune.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia belonged to which dynasty of the Maratha confederacy?
questions.explanation: Mahadji belonged to the Scindia (Shinde) dynasty, one of the four major Maratha chief-houses.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia expanded Maratha influence mainly in which part of India?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia restored and expanded Maratha influence across northern India, especially around Delhi.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia was the son of which founder of the Scindia house?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia was a son of Ranoji Scindia, who founded the Scindia dynasty under Baji Rao I.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia himself survived the Third Battle of Panipat in 1761 but was left with what?
questions.explanation: Mahadji was wounded at Panipat in 1761 and escaped with a permanent injury that left him lame.

questions.q: Which European officer famously organised and trained Mahadji Scindia's modern infantry brigades?
questions.explanation: The Savoyard officer Benoit de Boigne trained Mahadji Scindia's European-style infantry to great effect.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia restored which Mughal emperor to a measure of authority in Delhi, becoming his protector?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia became the protector of the Mughal emperor Shah Alam II at Delhi.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia secured for the Peshwa, and was himself granted, which prestigious Mughal title of regent?
questions.explanation: Mahadji obtained the office of Vakil-i-Mutlaq, deputy regent of the Mughal empire, for the Peshwa, acting as his deputy.

questions.q: In which year did Mahadji Scindia die?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia died in 1794 at Wanawadi near Pune.

questions.q: At the 1787 Battle of Lalsot, Mahadji Scindia faced a coalition led by which Rajput state?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia fought a Rajput coalition led by Jaipur and Jodhpur at the Battle of Lalsot in 1787.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia decisively defeated a Rajput-Mughal force at which 1790 battle, cementing his northern dominance?
questions.explanation: Mahadji's de Boigne-trained troops won the battles of Patan and Merta in 1790, confirming Scindia supremacy in the north.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia was succeeded by his grand-nephew (adopted heir), the ruler named what?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia, having no surviving son, was succeeded by his grand-nephew Daulat Rao Scindia.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia's power rested heavily on control of the strong fortress of what?
questions.explanation: Mahadji's authority in the north rested heavily on the great fortress of Gwalior, the Scindia stronghold.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia's modern army was notable for its strong arm of which weapon, cast in his own foundries?
questions.explanation: Mahadji built a powerful, disciplined artillery arm, casting cannon in foundries such as those at Agra.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia's main rival among the other Maratha houses for influence in the north was which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia and the Holkars were rival Maratha houses competing for primacy in northern India.

questions.q: In the disorder after Panipat, Mahadji Scindia restored Maratha control over Delhi by 1771 alongside which other Maratha commander?
questions.explanation: Mahadji helped lead the Maratha return to Delhi around 1771, restoring Shah Alam II with fellow commanders.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia negotiated the Treaty of Salbai in 1782, ending which war with the British?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia brokered the Treaty of Salbai in 1782, ending the First Anglo-Maratha War.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia had earlier helped defeat a British force at which 1779 engagement near Pune?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia played a key role in the Maratha success at Wadgaon in 1779, leading to the Convention of Wadgaon.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia obtained for the emperor's protection a famous title for himself; the European-trained corps he raised was sometimes called what?
questions.explanation: Mahadji's regular European-style brigades under de Boigne were known as the campoo or campo formations.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia died in 1794 at which place near Pune while attending to Maratha affairs?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia died at Wanawadi near Pune in 1794.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia was active in court politics during the Peshwa-ship of which young Peshwa, whom he supported through the regent Nana Phadnavis?
questions.explanation: Mahadji was a leading figure during the minority of Peshwa Madhavrao II, navigating Pune politics with Nana Phadnavis.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia's father Ranoji had first risen in service of which Peshwa, founding the family's fortunes?
questions.explanation: Ranoji Scindia, Mahadji's father, rose under Peshwa Baji Rao I, establishing the Scindia house.

questions.q: Mahadji Scindia extended Maratha control northwest, briefly bringing which strategic region near Delhi under Scindia influence?
options: The Doab and area up toward the Punjab borderlands
questions.explanation: Mahadji extended Scindia power across the Ganga-Yamuna Doab and toward the borderlands of the Punjab.
